name: Order Validation Simulation
description: Simulate the end-to-end deal desk and order validation process across 500 deals to identify bottlenecks, rejection patterns, and approval chain delays by segment and deal complexity.
seed_text:
Intercom's order validation pipeline processes every new deal and expansion through a multi-stage approval workflow before booking. Current state:

1. Deal desk reviews all deals above $10K ARR for pricing compliance, discount governance, and non-standard term approval
2. Legal review is triggered for custom DPA requests, SLA modifications, and multi-year commitments over $100K
3. Finance sign-off required for payment terms beyond net-30 or any deal with revenue recognition complexity
4. Security review triggered when prospects require custom data residency, HIPAA BAA, or FedRAMP documentation

Current pain points identified by RevOps:
- Average order validation cycle is 8.3 days, but P90 is 23 days — long-tail deals stall in legal/security
- 34% of deals require at least one revision loop back to the AE, adding 3-5 days each
- Discount approval thresholds are misaligned — 60% of escalations are for discounts between 15-25%, which are routinely approved
- Non-standard terms (custom SLAs, DPA addendums) account for 18% of deals but consume 52% of legal review capacity
- No visibility into which validation stage a deal is in — reps ping Slack channels for status updates
- Quarter-end surge: 40% of deals enter validation in the last 2 weeks of quarter, overwhelming the 3-person deal desk

Validation stages to simulate:
1. Automated pre-check: Pricing within published bands, standard terms, credit check
2. Deal desk review: Discount governance, competitive pricing, bundle validation
3. Legal review: Contract redlines, DPA/BAA, custom SLA terms
4. Security review: Data residency, compliance certifications, penetration test results
5. Finance approval: Payment terms, multi-year recognition, co-term alignment
6. Final booking: CPQ quote generation, Salesforce opportunity update, DocuSign trigger

Deal archetypes to test:
- Standard SMB ($5K-$20K ARR): Should auto-approve in <1 day
- Mid-Market with discount ($20K-$100K ARR, 15-30% discount): Deal desk + finance
- Enterprise custom ($100K-$500K ARR, non-standard terms): Full validation chain
- Strategic multi-year ($500K+ TCV, custom everything): All stages + executive sponsor
- Expansion/upsell (existing customer, co-term): Reduced validation but co-term complexity

The simulation should model 500 deals entering the pipeline simultaneously — varying by segment, deal complexity, discount depth, and term customization — and predict: cycle time distribution by archetype, stage-level bottleneck identification, revision loop frequency and root causes, quarter-end capacity modeling, and the revenue impact of validation delays on bookings targets.
expected_outputs: Cycle time distribution by deal archetype and validation stage
Bottleneck identification with stage-level dwell time analysis
Revision loop frequency and root cause categorization
Quarter-end capacity model with deal desk throughput limits
Revenue impact forecast from validation delays on bookings
Recommended discount threshold adjustments to reduce escalations
Auto-approval eligibility criteria for low-risk deal patterns
